name: openspec-daem0n-bridge
description: Bridges OpenSpec (spec-driven development) with Daem0n-MCP memory - auto-imports specs, informs proposals with past outcomes, converts archived changes to learnings
capabilities: Auto-detects OpenSpec directories and imports specifications to memory
Converts MUST and SHOULD requirements into enforceable rules
Provides relevant context from past decisions before creating proposals
Converts archived changes into learnings for future reference
Links related memories to create causal chains between decisions and outcomes
author: DasBluEyedDevil
securitySummary: All 95 static findings are false positives. This skill contains only markdown documentation describing integration workflows between OpenSpec and Daem0n-MCP. The detected patterns are code examples within markdown fenced blocks, not executable code. No actual shell execution, cryptographic operations, or security risks exist.
valueStatement: AI assistants often lack context about project specifications and past decisions. This skill syncs OpenSpec requirements to Daem0n memory, enabling informed proposals and preserving learnings.
userTitle: Bridge OpenSpec Specifications with Daem0n Memory
limitations: Requires existing OpenSpec directory structure in the project
Depends on Daem0n-MCP being installed and accessible
Only parses standard markdown spec file format

useCases.title: Maintain Specification Consistency
useCases.description: Ensure AI assistants reference current specifications when proposing changes. Reduce deviation from established patterns.

useCases.title: Track Implementation Outcomes
useCases.description: Convert completed implementations into learnings. Build organizational knowledge from past successes and failures.

useCases.title: Context-Aware Proposal Creation
useCases.description: Get warnings about past failures and relevant rules before proposing changes. Improve proposal success rates.

promptTemplates.title: Sync Specifications
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting work on a project with OpenSpec
promptTemplates.prompt: Sync all specifications from my openspec directory to Daem0n memory.

promptTemplates.title: Check Before Proposing
promptTemplates.prompt: I want to add OAuth support. What specifications and past outcomes should I consider before proposing this change?

promptTemplates.title: Record Archived Change
promptTemplates.prompt: I archived change AUTH-001. Please record the outcome and create learnings for future reference.

promptTemplates.title: Refresh Spec Memory
promptTemplates.prompt: Our specs have changed. Please refresh Daem0n memory with the latest OpenSpec updates.

bestPractices: Call get_briefing() at session start to enable automatic OpenSpec detection
Record proposal decisions before implementing to maintain an audit trail
Archive completed changes promptly to capture learnings while context is fresh
antiPatterns: Creating proposals without checking existing specifications and past outcomes
Manually editing memories created by this skill instead of using refresh commands
Skipping the archive step after completing changes which loses valuable learnings

faq.question: Is this compatible with all OpenSpec versions?
faq.answer: Yes, it works with standard OpenSpec markdown format. Custom formats may require pattern adjustments.

faq.question: What if Daem0n-MCP is not installed?
faq.answer: The skill will detect missing MCP tools and prompt you to install Daem0n-MCP first.

faq.question: Can I use this with multiple projects?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill maintains separate memory contexts per project based on current working directory.

faq.question: How are specification conflicts handled?
faq.answer: Each spec creates separate memories. Conflicts are surfaced during proposal creation for human resolution.

faq.question: What happens when specs change frequently?
faq.answer: Use the refresh command to update memories. Old versions are superseded, not deleted.

faq.question: Does this require an internet connection?
faq.answer: No. All operations are local. It reads local spec files and uses local Daem0n memory.
